items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: James Harden fails to show up for Cavaliers in Game 2 loss to Pistons
items.blurb: James Harden fails to show up for Cavaliers in Game 2 loss to Pistons — Two of the Cleveland Cavaliers' All-Stars showed up big in Game 2 of their second-round series. James Harden did not.

items.headline: Trail Blazers could be key to Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
items.blurb: Trail Blazers could be key to Giannis Antetokounmpo trade — No matter what kind of trade offers develop for the Greek Freak, the Blazers should be angling to involve themselves in the action.

items.headline: Draymond Green teases Warriors exit again amid Steve Kerr uncertainty
items.blurb: Draymond Green teases Warriors exit again amid Steve Kerr uncertainty — Green has been a defensive and emotional catalyst for the Warriors throughout the course of their dynasty.

items.headline: PJ Tucker Announces Retirement From NBA
items.blurb: PJ Tucker Announces Retirement From NBA — PJ Tucker announced his retirement from the NBA in an Instagram post on Thursday. "20 years being my job but 40 plus years of not being able to fathom doing anything other than it. So here's to retiring from the NBA... because I will NEVER stop ballin," wrote Tucker. Tucker played 19 seasons in the league with the Knicks, Raptors, Suns, Rockets, Bucks, Hea

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Says James Harden Will 'Figure It Out' After Scoring 10 Points In Game 2 Loss
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell Says James Harden Will 'Figure It Out' After Scoring 10 Points In Game 2 Loss — Donovan Mitchell said James Harden would "figure it out" following the Cleveland Cavaliers Game 2 loss to the Detroit Pistons . items.headline: ‘Worst whistle of any star player’: Redick says LeBron deserves more calls
items.blurb: ‘Worst whistle of any star player’: Redick says LeBron deserves more calls — After his team fell down 2-0 to the defending champs, Los Angeles Lakers head coach JJ Redick wasn’t hiding his frustration with the officiating.

items.headline: J.B. Bickerstaff Says Pistons 'Just Wear On You' Following Game 2 Win Over Cavs
items.blurb: J.B. Bickerstaff Says Pistons 'Just Wear On You' Following Game 2 Win Over Cavs — The Detroit Pistons defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 107-97 to win Game 2 and take a 2-0 series lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals. items.headline: Thunder literally steal Game 2 from Lakers with second-half surge
items.blurb: Thunder literally steal Game 2 from Lakers with second-half surge — The Thunder finished with as many steals as turnovers (12) as the Lakers coughed the ball up 21 times in the game.

items.headline: NBA playoffs winners, losers: Pistons, Thunder seize control with Game 2 victories
items.blurb: NBA playoffs winners, losers: Pistons, Thunder seize control with Game 2 victories — The second round of the 2026 NBA playoffs continued on Thursday night with the Detroit Pistons outlasting the Cleveland Cavaliers, 107-97, and the Oklahoma City Thunder pulling away late to down the Los Angeles Lakers, 125-107.

items.headline: Lakers’ Redick: LeBron has ‘worst whistle of any star player I’ve ever seen’
items.blurb: Lakers’ Redick: LeBron has ‘worst whistle of any star player I’ve ever seen’ — Los Angeles Lakers head coach JJ Redick discusses the team’s 125-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 2, expressing his frustration with the officiating and saying the LeBron James has the “worst whistle of any star player.”

items.headline: Chet Holmgren's ridiculous flop in Game 2 hurts Thunder's reputation
items.blurb: Chet Holmgren's ridiculous flop in Game 2 hurts Thunder's reputation — Chet Holmgren is one of the finest young big men in the NBA. In Game 2, he proved he was one of the NBA's finest actors as well.

items.headline: Holmgren, Gilgeous-Alexander score 22 as Thunder beat Lakers for 2-0 lead
items.blurb: Holmgren, Gilgeous-Alexander score 22 as Thunder beat Lakers for 2-0 lead — Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander each scored 22 points, and the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107 on Thursday night to take a 2-0 lead in the Western Conference semifinal series.

items.headline: LeBron finishes alley-oop, goes left off glass for dazzling Game 2 plays
items.blurb: LeBron finishes alley-oop, goes left off glass for dazzling Game 2 plays — 41-year-old Los Angeles Laker LeBron James produces a pair of special plays in the Lakers’ Game 2 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder, with a first quarter dunk and then a left-handed shot off the glass in the fourth quarter.

items.headline: NBA Playoff Highlights (May 8)
items.blurb: NBA Playoff Highlights (May 8)

items.headline: NBA Game 2 Highlights: Thunder 125, Lakers 107
items.blurb: NBA Game 2 Highlights: Thunder 125, Lakers 107 — Chet Holmgren scored 22 points, grabbed nine rebounds and recorded four steals as the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107 to take a 2-0 lead in their second round series.

items.headline: ‘Just fabulous’: Pistons’ Bickerstaff on Cunningham’s complete talent
items.blurb: ‘Just fabulous’: Pistons’ Bickerstaff on Cunningham’s complete talent — Detroit Pistons head coach J.B. Bickerstaff talks to the media after the team’s Game 2 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers about the skillset of Cade Cunningham and how the Pistons’ star guard impacts the game in so many ways on both ends of the court.

items.headline: Bucks reveal deadline for Giannis Antetokounmpo decision amid trade rumors
items.blurb: Bucks reveal deadline for Giannis Antetokounmpo decision amid trade rumors — Antetokounmpo and the Bucks have a decision to make.

items.headline: ‘On me’: Cavaliers’ Atkinson takes blame for Harden’s lack of touches
items.blurb: ‘On me’: Cavaliers’ Atkinson takes blame for Harden’s lack of touches — Cleveland Cavaliers head coach Kenny Atkinson speaks at his Game 2 post-game press conference and says that he needs to do a better job of putting James Harden in positions to succeed, as he shot only twice in the second half of the 107-97 loss.

items.headline: Pistons' Daniss Jenkins is NBA's unlikeliest playoff hero
items.blurb: Pistons' Daniss Jenkins is NBA's unlikeliest playoff hero — One of the most important players in the NBA playoffs attended four different colleges, went undrafted and didn't have an NBA contract until three months ago.

items.headline: NBA playoffs winners, losers: Spurs turn up defense on Wolves in blowout
items.blurb: NBA playoffs winners, losers: Spurs turn up defense on Wolves in blowout — Wednesday night in the NBA playoffs, defense reigned.

items.headline: Pistons, Cade Cunningham show Cavaliers, Donovan Mitchell who's boss in Game 2
items.blurb: Pistons, Cade Cunningham show Cavaliers, Donovan Mitchell who's boss in Game 2 — The Detroit Pistons are showing that they're in it to win it.

items.headline: Cunningham has 25 points, 10 assists to lift Pistons past Cavaliers for 2-0 lead
items.blurb: Cunningham has 25 points, 10 assists to lift Pistons past Cavaliers for 2-0 lead — Cade Cunningham had 25 points and 10 assists, Tobias Harris scored 21 points and the Detroit Pistons beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 107-97 on Thursday night to take a 2-0 lead in their second-round series.

items.headline: NBA Game 2 Highlights: Pistons 107, Cavaliers 97
items.blurb: NBA Game 2 Highlights: Pistons 107, Cavaliers 97 — Cade Cunningham registered a double-double of 25 points and 10 assists, Tobias Harris scored 21, and Daniss Jenkins added 14 points off the bench as the Detroit Pistons took a 2-0 lead against the Cleveland Cavaliers with a 107-97 win.

items.headline: How To Watch The WNBA
items.blurb: How To Watch The WNBA — The wait is finally over. Following a record-breaking 2025, the WNBA is stepping into its landmark 30th anniversary season with the momentum of a runaway freight train. Between a lucrative new media rights deal and the arrival of two fresh expansion teams, the 2026 campaign isn't just a season-it's a global takeover. items.blurb: The time is now: Jordan Clarkson needs to step up for Knicks — The last thing Knicks supporters want to see at this point is a key rotation player in street clothes. Good thing the Knicks have a backup plan.

items.blurb: Tiago Splitter Unlikely To Receive Full-Time Head Coaching Job With Blazers — Tiago Splitter, who guided the Portland Trail Blazers to a 42-39 record and the franchise's first playoff berth in five years, is unlikely to be offered the full-time head coaching position, sources with knowledge of the situation tell Jake Fischer. items.blurb: Cavaliers Contacted Bucks About Giannis Antetokounmpo Before Trade Deadline — The Cleveland Cavaliers reached out to the Milwaukee Bucks regarding a potential Giannis Antetokounmpo trade before the deadline, league sources say. Milwaukee's asking price included Evan Mobley and all of Cleveland's tradeable draft capital , which include three picks and three swaps on draft night. Multiple rival exec

items.headline: Charles Lee, Hornets Sign Contract Extension
items.blurb: Charles Lee, Hornets Sign Contract Extension — Charles Lee and the Charlotte Hornets have signed a contract extension. While the Hornets didn't make it out of the play-in and into the playoffs, they were one of the best teams in the NBA after a slow start to the 2025-26 season. The Hornets had a 25-win game improvement in Lee's second season as head coach. The Hornets hired Lee ahead of the 2024-2

items.blurb: OG Anunoby Day-To-Day With Hamstring Injury Ahead Of Game 3 At Sixers — New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby has been diagnosed with a right hamstring strain and will be monitored on a day-to-day basis, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania. items.headline: Knicks get massive OG Anunoby update ahead of Game 3 vs. 76ers
items.blurb: Knicks get massive OG Anunoby update ahead of Game 3 vs. 76ers — Anunoby missed the end of New York's Game 2 win.

items.blurb: Brad Stevens responds to Tracy McGrady's Jaylen Brown comments — Tracy McGrady is suggesting that Jaylen Brown has become frustrated with the Boston Celtics in the wake of their first-round playoff ouster.

items.blurb: Hornets sign coach Charles Lee to extension — The Hornets have signed head coach Charles Lee to a contract extension, the team announced Thursday.

items.headline: Knicks' playoff win streak accomplishes rarity for franchise
items.blurb: Knicks' playoff win streak accomplishes rarity for franchise — The New York Knicks' 108-102 win over the Philadelphia 76ers accomplished far more than just taking a 2-0 series lead in their second-round matchup.

items.headline: Jason Kidd's future could be in doubt after Mavericks hire Masai Ujiri
items.blurb: Jason Kidd's future could be in doubt after Mavericks hire Masai Ujiri — Jason Kidd wanted to be the team president of the Dallas Mavericks. Now the Mavs have hired Masai Ujiri, and the new president has stopped short of endorsing Kidd as his long-term head coach.

items.headline: AP Source: Knicks’ OG Anunoby day to day, questionable for Game 3
items.blurb: AP Source: Knicks’ OG Anunoby day to day, questionable for Game 3 — New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby will be listed as questionable for Game 3 against the Philadelphia 76ers on Friday (7:00 p.m. ET on Sportsnet 360 and Sportsnet+), ESPN’s Shams Charania reported Thursday.

items.headline: Chet Holmgren: Flirts with double-double in Game 2
items.blurb: Chet Holmgren: Flirts with double-double in Game 2 — Holmgren totaled 22 points (7-11 FG, 3-5 3Pt, 5-7 FT), nine rebounds, three assists, four steals and two blocks in 33 minutes during Thursday's 125-107 victory in Game 2 of the Western Conference Semifinals against the Lakers. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Austin Reaves: Pops for 31 points in Game 2
items.blurb: Austin Reaves: Pops for 31 points in Game 2 — Reaves finished with 31 points (10-16 FG, 3-6 3Pt, 8-10 FT), two rebounds and six assists across 38 minutes during Thursday's 125-107 loss in Game 2 of the Western Conference Semifinals against the Thunder. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Knicks' Mike Brown, Jalen Brunson react to OG Anunoby injury
items.blurb: Knicks' Mike Brown, Jalen Brunson react to OG Anunoby injury — The Knicks pulled off their second consecutive win in Game 2 of their Eastern Conference semifinal series against the Philadelphia 76ers on Wednesday.

items.blurb: Spurs Win By 38 In Game 2 To Even Series, Chris Finch Tells Wolves They 'Got Punked' — De'Aaron Fox and Victor Wembanyama rebounded from shaky Game 1 performances to power the San Antonio Spurs to a 133-95 blowout of the Minnesota Timberwolves on Wednesday night, evening the Western Conference semifinals at 1-1. items.headline: Cade Cunningham: Provides spark in fourth quarter
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham: Provides spark in fourth quarter — Cunningham produced 25 points (7-14 FG, 3-6 3Pt, 8-8 FT), four rebounds, 10 assists, one steal and two blocks across 42 minutes during Thursday's 107-97 victory in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals against Cleveland. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell: Puts up 31 points in loss
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell: Puts up 31 points in loss — Mitchell collected 31 points (11-24 FG, 2-9 3Pt, 7-9 FT), six rebounds, three assists and two steals over 37 minutes during Thursday's 107-97 loss to the Pistons in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Ayo Dosunmu: Questionable for Game 3
items.blurb: Ayo Dosunmu: Questionable for Game 3 — Dosunmu (heel) is questionable for Friday's Game 3 of the Western Conference Semifinals against the Spurs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
